entry.title: Re: Interact programmatically with SAP CAP framework's No...
entry.content: Please check https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/advanced/hybrid-testing#cds-bind-exec

entry.title: What’s New for SAP Integration Suite – August 2024
entry.content:
Fall is coming and the next TechEd Virtual is already in preparation. Take the chance and flip through the session catalog for all the interesting sessions of our integration track. Register now and join us online October 8-9, 2024. 

As usual find with this blogpost the highlights of our SAP Integration Suite increments that have been released in August:

  • Get ready with custom and purchased adapters for the Apache Camel 3.14 upgrade
  • Edit namespace for message types
  • Persistence in MariaDB databases
  • Principal propagation as authentication method to connect with on-premise systems
  • New parameters in SFTP and AMQP sender adapters
  • Inspection of content size
  • Diagnostic tasks in operation cockpit of Edge Integration Cell
  • Community integration package for extension points
  • Appropriate time zone in B2B monitor
  • Suborganizations in company profiles
  • Support of EDI flat-file payloads
  • Integration Advisor migration guide
  • Envelope templates for EDI messages
  • New version of the Process Integration pipeline concept
  • Adapter modules
  • Manage content from API business hub enterprise
  • Default credential type for service key

A2A integration

Get ready with custom and purchased adapters for the Apache Camel 3.14 upgrade

As the Apache Camel upgrade is happening soon, do some prerequisites to run your integration scenarios before your productive tenants are updated to the new runtime. Please upload a Camel 3.x compatible version of your custom adapters and of adapters you have purchased from other vendors to your productive tenant. A special area is available for upload. By uploading your adapters, we ensure that they will get ported automatically during the update.

With regards to OEM adapters by partners, please run the latest Camel 2.x compatible versions. An upload isn’t necessary.

To avoid any business disruption with unexpected integration flow behaviour, we recommend an upfront validation of your custom adapters. For this purpose you may leverage the free 90 days valid test tenant that already offer for quite some time. It runs on Apache Camel 3.14 where you can test your adapters and integration flows.

Ensure that also your design time has the latest version of the adapters and upload the .esa files to the appropriate integration packages. For more details, see SAP Note #3326553.

Edit namespace for message types

The recently introduced message types have been extended in the way that you can now view the namespace to which a message type belongs to and edit and create a new one. Refer the documentation.

Persistence in MariaDB databases

To support our SAP Process Orchestration customers, we have extended the Java database connectivity (JDBC) receiver adapter by supporting now also MariaDB. The adapter is available in multi-cloud environments and in Edge Integration Cell runtime for ground-to-ground integration scenarios. See the documentation.

Principal propagation as authentication method to connect with on-premise systems

You can now propagate the principal of the inbound technical user with the principal propagation authentication method in the HTTP receiver adapter. The tenant authenticates itself against the receiver by forwarding the principal of the inbound user to the Cloud Connector, and from there to the back end of the relevant on-premise system. Check out the documentation.

New parameters in SFTP and AMQP sender adapters

We have introduced new parameters in the SFTP and AMQP adapters. The SFTP sender adapter is extended by Poll on one worker only used to specify that the polling process is performed on a single worker node only. The AMQP sender adapter that is consuming messages from queues in an external message broker offers now the parameter Disable Reply-To allowing you to disable the reply back for a message.

Inspection of content size

The powerful inspect feature to analyze your resource consumption has been extended. You can now inspect the file size of all the integration artifacts (such as integration flows, value mappings, adapters) they allocate on the tenant they are deployed on. You may detect the top consumers and in case of integration flow artifacts switch also between the various inspect categories to detect the consumption of other relevant database and system resources. Refer the Inspect Content Size.

 

Integration in private landscapes

Diagnostic tasks in operation cockpit of Edge Integration Cell

Integration administrators can run diagnostic tasks in the operation cockpit of Edge Integration Cell. You may increase the log level configuration temporary with only few clicks, and with the Takt 2408 in September you may either trigger and view thread and heap dump on Java components. The diagnostic data can be collected on a file system and shared with SAP Support for troubleshooting. Check out the documentation.

B2B integration

Community integration package for extension points

The B2B Integration Factory integration packages are available for customers that require additional extension points and custom features that are not yet provided by the standard TPM solution. If the standard TPM solution does not cover your integration requirements, use the community packages instead.

The following packages are available:

  • Cloud Integration - Trading Partner Management
  • Communication Receiver Flows - Custom
  • Communication Sender Flows
  • Interchange Extraction Flows
  • Extended Interchange Processing Flows
  • Pre and Post-Processing Flows - Custom

Appropriate time zone in B2B monitor

To enhance efficiency in B2B monitoring, dates and times are now displayed according to your browser's time zone, rather than UTC. Additionally, you can easily rearrange field positions using drag and drop, and sort the columns in the interchange list. For more information, see monitor interchanges and monitor unassigned interchanges.

Suborganizations in company profiles

Within the Trading Partner Management subsidiaries are supported. You can now create and maintain suborganization profiles as children of the main company profile. They may have their own configuration requirements, such as certificates, systems, and communication channels. Check out the documentation for more details.

Support of EDI flat-file payloads

For payload XML was the only supported format by the Integration Advisor capability so far. To increase your efficiency, you can now use the text-based flat-file EDI format (used for X12, UN/EDIFACT, and Tradacoms) as well. EDI payloads can be uploaded as MIGs, and you can also use the payload during the MIG and MAG simulation. The conversion of the EDI payload into its XML representation is implicitly done by Integration Advisor. Check out all the details in the blogpost.

Integration Advisor migration guide

We have now published a migration guide for the Integration Advisor capability that details the steps needed for customer planning to migrate their design time artifacts from the Neo environment to SAP Integration Suite.

Envelope templates for EDI messages

The EDI standards X12 and UN/EDIFACT have so called envelopes that are wrapped around the actual messages. You can now define Message Implementation Guidelines (MIGs) that combine envelope and message structure and share this documentation with business partners. For the definition you can leverage envelope templates as defined by the standards. The envelope template content will be released gradually over the next few weeks, starting with UN/EDIFACT and ASC X12, followed by EDIFACT subsets and Odette. See the details in the documentation.

Migration from SAP Process Orchestration

New version of the Process Integration pipeline concept

To run your asynchronous message processing in Cloud Integration in a similar way as you do it in SAP Process Orchestration, we have introduced the pipeline concept. Now we have released a version 1.0.6 of the package with some enhancements. To offer a way to define sender wildcard scenarios we have revised the partner ID definition and support also alternative partners to overcome partner id restrictions. Another enhancement is that the receiver and interface determination can now be bypassed in pure point-to-point scenarios to improve the runtime performance. Read the very detailed blog post and see the pipeline package published at the SAP Business Accelerator Hub. And if you like to make yourself familiar check out the Process Integration pipeline sample scenarios in the GitHub repository including also a guideline how to  the pipeline concept

Adapter modules

The message transformation utilities community package contains artifacts that help transforming message payloads to various formats, such as XML, plain text, CSV, and JSON. The package delivers various functionalities that once existed in SAP Process Orchestration as adapter modules. The utilities covered so far are MessageTransformBean and XMLAnonymizer.

API-managed integration

Manage content from API business hub enterprise

You can now publish selected SAP system APIs directly into the API business hub enterprise catalog, without first defining API proxies for these APIs. To accomplish this, your global account administrator first needs to register these systems in your tenant. The registered systems then appear in the manage content page of API business hub enterprise, from which you can then publish the APIs directly. See the documentation.

Default credential type for service key

In the API business hub enterprise, we've updated the default credential type for the service key. It's now binding-secret instead of instance-secret. We made this change because xsuaa has deprecated the credential type instance-secret. See more details in our documentation.

How to stay tuned to recent and upcoming innovations?

The SAP Road Map Explorer is your one-stop shop for all SAP Integration Suite innovations. You can easily check out the latest innovations and follow what is planned for the following quarters. All recent innovations also cover under the tab Features further links to blogposts or documentation.

We also refer to the complete list of new releases in our documentation: What’s New in SAP Integration Suite.

And if you have not heard of our monthly webinars, I suggest you sign up to get an invitation to the upcoming ones. Our team of Product Management experts host these webinars to showcase the latest and greatest updates regarding all SAP Integration Suite capabilities. The webinars are hosted on the last Tuesday of every month and the next one is already scheduled for September 24th.

In case you have missed our last monthly webinar, don’t worry. Visit 2024 Learning Sessions for SAP User Groups on SAP Integration Suite for all recordings, PDFs, and Q&As.

Are you aware of the Release Navigator for SAP BTP? It consolidates release information across SAP BTP products and services easing you the way to find product release related notes, blogposts, and webpages. For your convenience use the direct link to the SAP Integration Suite section of the Release Navigator.

entry.title: Re: BPA - Process Automation for Project Team Assignments
entry.content:
Hello Hanumanth,

Thank you for reaching out to us. Our expert, Jakob, has addressed most of your queries. Are you still encountering any unresolved issues? Additionally, I recommend following our help portal for SAP Build Process Automation  - https://help.sap.com/docs/build-process-automation?locale=en-US  for any further questions.

entry.title: Re: Special Release Linux on IBM Power Little Endian for SAP NetWeaver 7.4 application servers
entry.content: You need a S-USER and access the portal SAP for Me.

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One – Formatted Searches and How to Find Them
entry.content:
More than welcome Santos - glad to hear it helped out.  I also have first-hand knowledge of how frustrating it can be when doing a migration and trying to manually figure all of this out.  The main reason it was written.

Regards to you in Brazil - Zal  &:-D

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio Process Accelerator VS other SAP Info So...
entry.content:
Dear JD_WongLoera, 

thank you for your question and apologies for the delay in response. As this is such an extensive question related to different sources of information, it is difficult to answer it in a concise manner in this threat with all the information you desire. 

I would like to point you to our upcoming webcast How to leverage SAP Signavio Value Accelerators with the Value Accelerator Library on the 24th of September 2024. This would be the perfect opportunity to exchange with our experts also on the differences in the content as raised by you. 

You can also find a blog on the Value Accelerator Library in our Community and interact with our experts there. 

Please note, to access the webcast link, a one-time registration in the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by-step guide to registration can be found here.

Best regards, 

Katie

entry.title: Free Of Charge Process Public Cloud Sales
entry.content:
Hello,

I am using the CBXN item type for the CBFD document type in the Free of charge process. In my pricing schema, there are D100 conditions with routines 55 and 28. Subsequently, I want to create a proforma for delivery using F8, but the amount is not showing as 0. Is there any alternative routine I could use?

entry.title: Re: Details of PR not showing on MY Fiori Inbox SAP S4/HANA
entry.content: Hi, Yes you need to maintain that entry for your workflow ID which is starting with WS.

entry.title: How to Import an UBL XSD Structure in SAP ERP 6.0
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

 

I have a request to transform an invoice into an XML file; however, this XML must comply with the UBL standard. I understand that with the transaction STRANS I can include a Transformation to fill a structure automatically, nevertheless, When I see the UBL XSD Schema, to create a structure manually on the Tcode SE11, seems to be a wrong approach. Does anyone know a way to include or upload the UBL XSD Schema for the automatic creation of the structure in the DDIC ? If someone can send me a step by step, or any Information, it will be very appreciated. 

 

In some threads I red that with Eclipse is also possible, unfortunately I'm not familiarized with Eclipse 😞 

 

Thanks a lot in advance,

Lee

entry.title: Re: Solman_setup 500 Internal Server Error
entry.content: Thanks for your reply but it was hostname parameter issue. But still thanks for your time. Hope you have great day

entry.title: Re: Excel Upload of Test Case into SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: Thanks Ajay, this is really helpful, pls let us now once mass upload is released..

entry.title: Remove 1st rehire verification check and only use the 2nd rehire verification?
entry.content:
Has anyone removed the 1st rehire verification check and only used the 2nd rehire verification check?  We only want to have a rehire verification done once the candidate has provided their personal data.  We don't want the system to perform the 1st rehire verification check as it holds up the candidate getting their welcome email and delays the onboarding process.

Thanks 

Kristi Thomas

entry.title: Re: Using Signavio Process Governance Event Data in Proce...
entry.content:
Dear spartan_2000,

thank you for your question. 

The automatic transfer of data from SAP Signavio Process Governance into SAP Signavio Process Intelligence (or SAP Signavio Process Insights) is currently not possible. We would be more than happy to add this as a feature request for our product team to evaluate. 

To do so, could you please send an email to signavio_success@sap.com with your request and the benefits / your specific use case for this feature. As described in your post the benefit would be the possibility to analyze the data on a real-time basis rather than importing data manually ever so often. Any further information you can provide for this feature to be beneficial for you will help our product team to understand and evaluate the feature. 

Thank you so much in advance, 

Katie

entry.title: Re: External Budget Check Response Failed
entry.content: Try to diable CIG and Re-enable CIG,  this should refresh the endpoints in the backend.

entry.title: Re: Cloud Integration: Call iFlow | Basic Authentication | user from Azure Active Directory | IAS as
entry.content: @CarlosRoggan, at the beginning of this post you mention the case of iFlow endpoints being protected with OAuth.  In that scenario, would there be any security reasons to add an API overlay to the same OAuth-protected endpoint using SAP API Management?

entry.title: Re: Filter Value help results in Child entity based on parent entity field value
entry.content:
You can achieve this through 'default function' functionality. In  bdef of your parent entity, define default function to pass the parent field value to associated child. then This will by default filter the value help result based on parent entity field. Example:

Step1. Add association  _childEntity { create{ default function GetDefaultsForChildEntity;} with draft; }

Step 2. Define this method in Behv Impl Class

  METHODS GetDefaultsForChildEntity FOR READ

IMPORTING keys FOR FUNCTION ParentEntity~GetDefaultsForChildEntity RESULT result.

Step 3. implement this function in behv Impl class.      

READ ENTITIES OF r_ParentEntity

IN LOCAL MODE ENTITY ParentEntity(Alias of parent entity)

FIELDS ( Parent_entity_field_name )

WITH CORRESPONDING #( keys )

RESULT DATA(lt_data).

CHECK lt_data[ 1 ] IS NOT INITIAL.

result = VALUE #( %param-Parent_entity_field_name = lt_data[ 1 ]-Parent_entity_field_name

( %tky = CORRESPONDING #( keys[ 1 ] ) ) ).

 

Step-3. Done. this will by default filter child entity VH result based on value of Parent_entity_field_name.

entry.title: Re: RAP add table in ObjectPage
entry.content: Does that target cds have lineitem annotation?

entry.title: Re: I want to connect SAC to my on premise SAP HANA database
entry.content: It would help if you share what connection method you use (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ANALYTICS_CLOUD/00f68c2e08b941f081002fd3691d86a7/cc03773d7f554c4ebc9e29b4c2792e26.html?version=release&locale=en-US) and what error message you are getting.

entry.title: Re: How to release a retention payment during Payment Run
entry.content: Thank you for the help here. We are actually inputting a Retention Due Date during the IR but when we run normal payment processing after that, it is not picking up the invoice.

entry.title: Re: Duplicidade dados NF entrada - J1B1N
entry.content:
Solução standard:

Aplicar SAP notes: 1614648 e 1606367 (INCLUDE LJ1BB2FF0) 

Atualizar visão T160M com a mensagem 8B 669.

Ao gravar a NF, irá mostrar a mensagem.

entry.title: Re: Finally! we have an open source LLM trained on SAP APIs and ABAP
entry.content:
Hi @SonicPlanet 

> Is there the capability to use ABAP or HANA SQL for embeddings using the vector database?

Please check https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-hana-cloud-vector-engine-quick-faq-reference/ba-p/13675212 by @shabana

entry.title: SAML2 service not accessibleWhat has happened?Calling of URL https://myxxxx.scmibp1.ondemand.c
entry.content:
SAML2 service not accessible

What has happened?

Calling of URL https://myxxxx.scmibp1.ondemand.c was terminated during SAML2 processing

 Note

  • No RelayState mapping found for RelayState value oucxzzvvwvaoewtboreefozabfoyazwwzyeawuw

 

HTTP 404 Not Found

© 2001-2024, SAP SE

entry.title: Can we replace the existing FSV with new one OR Can we overwrite FSV's ?
entry.content:
I copied an existing FSV and created a new one with a different name. After making changes to the new FSV, I'm trying to overwrite the original FSV from which the new one was created. Is this possible?

#FSV

#FSE2

#OB58

SAP HANA 

SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Cálculo DIFAL Base Dupla
entry.content:
Pode implementar a regra utilizando a BADI:
DIFAL - BADI_J1B_DIFAL_RECALC - Método RECALCULATE

entry.title: Re: PO not sent to ariba network
entry.content:
Hello,

The error could appear if the trading relationship with your supplier was interrupted by them. Try resending the supplier a TRR (https://me.sap.com/notes/KB0395260) and then resend the order, which can be done via Enhanced Resending of Purchase Orders Stuck in Ordering or Cancelling State (https://me.sap.com/notes/KB0717382).

entry.title: Re: Devtoberfest 2024 Contest
entry.content: I am excited to participate, this is my first year. 😁

entry.title: Re: RAP add table in ObjectPage
entry.content: No, the target view has no UI annotations

entry.title: Re: Cálculo DIFAL Base Dupla
entry.content: *BADI_J1B_DIFAL_RECALCULATION

entry.title: Re: External storage permission in android
entry.content:
Hello Marajah,

Thank you for reaching out to us.

To remove the permission, you can use the App_Resources_Merge functionality:

  1. Create the following file under your .mdkproject folder:
css
Copy code
<yourproject>.mdkproject/App_Resources_Merge/Android/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml
  1. Add the following content to the AndroidManifest.xml:
xml
Copy code
<manifest package="__PACKAGE__" android:versionCode="1" android:versionName="1.0" xmlns:android="http://schemas.android.com/apk/res/android" xmlns:tools="http://schemas.android.com/tools"> <uses-permission android:name="android.permission.MANAGE_EXTERNAL_STORAGE" tools:node="remove"/> </manifest>
  1. Finally, rebuild your client using the create-client command.

Let me know if you need any further assistance.

entry.title: Re: Nota Fiscal avulsa x chave de acesso

entry.title: Re: Abap program to end specific User's Program, by sesion/tcode or GUIID
entry.content: 😎Look at the already provided include TSKHINCL

entry.title: Potenzial entfalten mit dem SAP Builder Day: Low-Code-Entwicklung mit SAP Build
entry.content:
Bereit, die Entwicklungsfähigkeiten auf die nächste Stufe zu heben? Egal, ob erfahren oder gerade erst am Anfang, der SAP Builder Day: Low-Code-Entwicklung mit SAP Build am 26. September und 12. November 2024 bietet die perfekte Gelegenheit, Fähigkeiten zu erweitern und Ideen zum Leben zu erwecken.

Warum am SAP Builder Day teilnehmen?

  1. Beschleunigte Lernkurve

Low-Code-Entwicklung ist die Zukunft. Dieser Kurs bietet einen schnellen, effizienten Weg, um die wesentlichen Konzepte von SAP Build zu erlernen und innerhalb kurzer Zeit Fähigkeiten zu erwerben, die normalerweise Wochen des traditionellen Lernens erfordern.

  1. Praxiserfahrung

Theorie ist gut, aber Praxis ist Gold wert. Der SAP Builder Day besteht nicht nur aus Demos, sondern auch aus praktischen Übungen, realen Anwendungsfällen und interaktiven Sitzungen, sodass anwendbare Kenntnisse gewonnen werden können.

  1. Expertenanleitung

Direkt von SAP-Experten lernen, die jahrelange Branchenerfahrung und tiefes Produktwissen mitbringen. Fragen werden in Echtzeit beantwortet, wodurch wertvolle Einblicke gewonnen werden können.

Inhalte des Kurses

  • Einführung in die SAP Build-Tools: Ein umfassender Überblick und sofortiger Praxisbezug.
  • Praktische Anwendungen: Erstellung funktionaler Anwendungen in kurzer Zeit.
  • Best Practices: Lernen der Dos and Don'ts der Low-Code-Entwicklung für effiziente und skalierbare Projekte.

Die Gelegenheit nicht verpassen!

Dies ist mehr als nur ein Kurs; es ist eine karriereverändernde Möglichkeit. Die Plätze sind begrenzt, also nicht warten – jetzt Platz für den 26. September und 12. November 2024 sichern!

👉Jetzt anmelden

entry.title: Re: RAP add table in ObjectPage
entry.content:
https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com/sdk/#/topic/d869d7ab3caa48b2a20dc20dfa248380.html

if your app is v4, the standard change document component can only be integrated as popup screen.

entry.title: Real Talk with Stephanie and Konstantin | SAP Business Technology Platform Track Leads
entry.content:
We are Konstantin Heine and Stephanie Stang, Product Managers for SAP BTP. We look forward to the upcoming SAP TechEd Virtual – especially towards the SAP BTP General track.

Konstantin: As a child, I always played with interlocking building blocks. This led to my career, which now has to do with a modular platform. But jokes aside, I find it incredibly fascinating to see the multifaceted ways SAP BTP is utilized. In my role, I focus on making the development and operation of applications on SAP BTP easier, while also providing supporting assets to navigate certain challenges.

Stephanie:  While taking care of the SAP BTP cross roadmap topics and creating awareness for our customers, I discover every day what you can get out of SAP BTP. But even in private, I like to discover a lot. That’s why I like travelling around the world and getting to experience new adventures.

The SAP Business Technology Platform track is designed to provide an overview of the key technologies that make up the platform. We cover a broad range of topics and grouped them into three sub-tracks that help you to easily find sessions of interest:

  1. Strategy & Overview – Starting point to learn everything about SAP BTP and strategic topics.
  2. Guidance & Operations – Content on how to get started or advanced content on guidance and best practices.
  3. Security & Compliance – Contains sessions that help you understand how to securely operate and integrate your applications.

What inspired you to specialize in this track?

Our track provides insights into future trends with guidance on architecture, methodologies, and governance models to help define your path forward. Every SAP BTP application requires guidance for architecture, operations, and security. Regular discussions on these topics with customers and partners further enhance and improve our content.

How does this track embody the ‘where ideas get real’ spirit? 

In our SAP BTP General track, you’ll discover technology innovations that bring ideas to life. Sessions offer an overview of the platform’s key technologies, getting started guidance, best practices, and how to operate and securely integrate your applications. Begin with hands-on experience in the SAP Discovery Center or explore reference architectures within the SAP BTP Guidance Framework, covering your SAP BTP journey end-to-end.

Track stars (top sessions to keep on your radar)

  1. XP101 - SAP BTP strategy and highlights: See how SAP BTP brings together all pillars and get insights into our development priorities, explore upcoming innovation highlights, and come away with a clear picture of the platform’s future direction.
  2. XP105 - Start your journey with SAP BTP and confidently accelerate adoption: Learn how to get started with SAP BTP and what assets exist to design, develop, and operate enterprise-grade applications on SAP BTP.
  3. XP103 - Got security? Services for security and compliance with SAP BTP: Explore the services SAP BTP offers around security as well as identity and access management. 
  4. XP201 - Observability of custom-built CAP applications with SAP Cloud ALM: Learn how you can instrument your custom applications based on SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP), using OpenTelemetry.
  5. XP180 - Get started with SAP BTP: Discover and accelerate with SAP Discovery Center: This Jump-Start session allows you to explore the latest enhancements in speed of project implementation and personalization in SAP Discovery Center. 

And don’t miss the Track Overview session with both of us: XP100 - Unlock business innovation and achieve long-term success with SAP BTP.  Together, we will deep-dive into the sessions and discuss some of the highlights presented throughout the track.

What should be in everyone’s TechEd toolkit for 2024?

  • Coffee: Could be quite useful managing to watch the broad range of TechEd sessions.
  • Agenda Builder: Allows you to build your individual agenda and avoid colliding sessions (this will probably happen, but don’t worry as you can watch the recordings later).
  • Excitement and a lot of questions: Make sure to use the Q&A tool during sessions. We will be answering questions live in the session and in the tool.

Excited for what you will gain out of our track? Then join us at SAP TechEd Virtual in October and register today.

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Inspection lot creation on Sales Return Ins...
entry.content: @ArnoBrouwer Yes Sir same setting is there still creating the Non-stock relevant Inspection lot

entry.title: Re: How to migrate data with CDS view behaviour LTMOM
entry.content: Hi Sybille, thank you very much for the help. I tried to migrate with A_BANK_3 but without succes. I did all the steps you described. I also used the same test banks in the standard migration object for banks. There I didnt get any Errors. Is there any way to check if I use the correct view behaviour?

entry.title: Re: Summaries containing string
entry.content:
Given that InvalidNumberCheck is a formula, you should refer to it as {@InvalidNumberCheck} rather than  {InvalidNumberCheck}

Better yet, use the formula editor to navigate and insert it into the formula expression, instead of trying to type it yourself.

entry.title: Re: Custom ABAP CDS Views in Public Cloud
entry.content: When I try to create a CDS is there a process or step-by-step instruction available?

entry.title: Re: When and How to contact the SAP ID Service team?
entry.content: Great job Ciara, well done!! 🙂

entry.title: Re: 3 Decimal places for Data Migration in S4 Public cloud
entry.content:
Hello Dineshkumar,

for which objects do have this problem? Only for Open items AP or also for AR and G/L open items?

BR

Frank

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Purchase Requisitions
entry.content:
Hello 

Issues where MRP generates a new purchase requisition are very often related to the rescheduling check. If your purchase order is outside the rescheduling horizon or not included in the rescheduling check, MRP might have to create a new purchase requisition to cover the reorder point. This WIKI talks about the rescheduling check:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/mrp/3138697863.html

Another possibility is that your purchase order is not relevant to MRP. If the available quantity column is blank for your PO in transaction MD04, it means that it is not relevant to MRP, and that's why a new requisition can be created.

Regards,

Caetano

entry.title: Re: SAP Open Connectors With Cloud Integration Scenario
entry.content:
Hello,

can you explain why you haven't used the OpenConnectors adapter in the integration flow?

Best Regards
Gregor

entry.title: Re: HXEDownloadManager can't run on windows 10
entry.content: For the platform independent version (.jar), it requires Java. The command to start it is java -jar HXEDownloadManager.jar.

entry.title: Re: When and How to contact the SAP ID Service team?
entry.content: Well explained blog, thank you Ciara! 😉

entry.title: SAP Enterprise Support Academy Newsletter September 2024
entry.content:
Dear customers,
Find here an overview of the SAP Enterprise Support Academy monthly newsletter on latest news, training courses, upcoming sessions, and further educational offerings.
The SAP Enterprise Support Academy provides expert guidance and high impact knowledge transfer services that enable you to innovate and be successful with your SAP solutions.

Subscribe to regularly receive latest news and stay informed about the wide range of educational offerings provided by the SAP Enterprise Support Academy that help you to build the digital skills tailored to your needs.

Preview available live sessions on SAP Support Portal, tailored per region:
APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America*

Please note: A one-time registration for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support [accessible here] is necessary to register for these events.

E-Learning: SAP Datasphere Lifecycle Management

With this new E-Learning, you are guided through how lifecycle management works in SAP Datasphere, and what is involved with maintenance of spaces. The bite-size video format allows you to work through the topics at your own pace, with expert insights, all in under 90 minutes.

Full details in this blog post

Get ready with SAP Readiness Check for SAP S/4HANA

Learn all about the SAP Readiness Check tool for SAP S/4HANA, and how it supports a successful conversion to SAP S/4HANA. We offer short and interactive live must-know sessions to get you prepared to start your SAP S/4HANA project.
We offer two sessions on September 10 and 13, one in English and one in Spanish: Register here

Gain the Skills Needed for Your Journey With SAP S/4HANA

Make yourself ready to take the next step on your SAP S/4HANA conversion. Just join one of our five interactive live trainings about SAP S/4HANA. The sessions are short, cover all relevant information, and are offered in several languages.

Pick the topic most relevant to you and register today

See What’s New and Popular

Overwhelmed by the wide range of offerings? Get inspired by customers most popular courses and check out which learnings are newly available in SAP Learning Hub.

Most Popular Learning Offerings
New Learning Items

Live Sessions – SAP Cloud ALM | Security | Data Volume Management

Join the upcoming knowledge transfer session on the value maps for SAP Cloud ALM | Security | Data Volume Management. Find related self-paced learnings and expert-led live sessions that are available through the value maps. Ask questions and get guidance on how our Foundational Support offering can help you achieve your project goals.

Save September 11 and register here

Q3 Updates to the Release Navigator for SAP Customer Experience

Release Navigator for SAP Customer Experience ensures you're current on the most recent updates and functionalities of your SAP CX solutions. The newest update showcase a redesigned interface along with the latest release details and content.

We invite you to read more about the changes we made in a blog post or explore the release navigator right away.

Compatibility Packs in SAP S/4HANA: The Way Forward

If you are live on SAP S/4HANA or SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and using classic standard functionality such as LE-WM Basic Warehousing or FI-TV Travel and Expense, then you are probably already aware of compatibility packs. SAP S/4HANA compatibility packs are temporary use rights to run certain classic SAP ERP solutions in SAP S/4HANA. The use of these compatibility packs will expire at the end of 2025, so it is important to start planning for the transition now. This involves evaluating your current usage of compatibility packs and determines how to migrate to the new standard functionalities in SAP S/4HANA.

SAP S/4HANA value maps help you to get an understanding of compatibility packs in SAP S/4HANA, including their temporary usage rights and the implications for users. Learn about important end-of-support deadlines, effective transition strategies, and critical considerations for a smooth migration to the latest SAP S/4HANA releases.

  • Value Map Learning Journeys
  • Value Map Blog Post Directory (value maps access required)
  • “Compatibility Packs in SAP S/4HANA” E-learning

Newly Designed Case List in SAP for Me

Check out the improved user experience on case management. Learn about the new UI and how to create a personalized view.
sap.to/61109Yi9yD

Transforming Customer Experience with AI

In this episode Becky Doyle, Head of Technical Support – SAP SuccessFactors HCM, shares with us some of the recent AI-powered product innovations that are transforming the customer experience when using our SAP SuccessFactors HCM solutions to one of faster access into decision-critical insights. In addition, Built-In Support allows the SAP SuccessFactors HCM solution itself to become an extension of Real-Time Support. Join us as we preview an exciting time for our HCM customers and how we support them. You don’t want to miss this episode!

Check out here

Customer Retention: Best Solutions For Real Results

In this exclusive Q&A, Stefan Steinle, executive vice president and head of Customer Support, SAP SE, shares more about how customer support teams can harness the power of cutting-edge tech like artificial intelligence to help bring innovative improvements and deliver value to businesses.

Read more

Customer Support at SAP: Partnering with Customers to Maximize Value for Their Businesses

From its inception, SAP’s Customer Support has stood out for its expanded role beyond bug fixes and issue resolutions. Daily tasks include solving tickets and resolving issues, all aimed at maximizing customer business value, securing investments, and optimizing SAP operations. We are a proactive business partner committed to helping customers achieve their goals.

sap.to/61109Y2d8B

Serving Customers Better Lean Selective Data Transition

The SAP Business Transformation Center streamlines the data transition from SAP ERP 6.0 to SAP S/4HANA. It is deployed on SAP Cloud ALM and is included in RISE with SAP or any other SAP cloud solution and is ready to use within 30 minutes. Its standout feature, the lean selective data transfer or “magic button”, simplifies data migration by allowing company codes to be left behind. This ensures that only useful data is transferred, speeding up the process. In combination with the digital blueprint, it provides end-to-end support that significantly reduces costs and time.

Click here

Customer References: Vodafone / Precision Drilling / Arthrex / NTT DATA

Vodafone: Supporting Software Implementation
Vodafone was able to have a successful go-live with support from SAP Mission Control Center. Read the full quote: sap.to/61106kBrbr

Precision Drilling: Successfully addressing obstacles
Learn how Precision Drilling was able to optimize test planning and execution, enhance communication channels, and deliver comprehensive reporting capabilities using SAP Cloud ALM and RISE with SAP. sap.to/61103cLAZX

Arthrex: Real-Time Support gains faster issue resolution
Learn how Arthrex was able to resolve issues faster with the Expert Chat service and SAP Support Portal. sap.to/61101cutiN

NTT DATA: Increasing the Efficiency of Data Center Operations
NTT DATA Business Solutions implemented SAP Focused Run and achieved significant improvements in data center efficiency. They migrated thousands of systems from multiple landscapes, previously managed by SAP Solution Manager, to a centralized, and efficient management platform under SAP Focused Run.
Read the full quote: sap.to/61105kBRXT

  • Guidance on signing up
  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy Video
  • SAP Enterprise Support Academy at a glance
  • Direct access to SAP Learning Hub (Login with your s-user ID and password)
  • SAP ES Academy Entitlement document
  • FAQ
  • SAP Enterprise Support value maps
  • SAP Support Portal
  • Basics: Sign up | Time zone setup |
  • Formats: EGI | MTE | Program
  • Search: Language | Keyword | Filter | Calendar
  • Upcoming live sessions in your region: APJ | EMEA | Latin America | North America

entry.title: Re: How to release a retention payment during Payment Run
entry.content: Can you check open vendor items, look for special GL indicator related to retentions, look for due date also, insure to select those in Outbound Payment Customizing and during payment run.

entry.title: Re: New Sandbox Available on the SAP Learning Site to Practice Hands-on
entry.content: Will the learning hub subscription allow me to utlize APIs to pull the sample data to and from my instance of S/4HANA?

entry.title: Intelligent Automation for SAP Business ByDesign: Bots Available
entry.content:
SAP Business ByDesign has introduced a suite of intelligent automation bots designed to streamline processes, reduce manual workloads, and enhance accuracy across various business functions.

You can refer to the below help documentation which briefs about the automation bots: 

Working with SAP Best Practices for Intelligent Automation for SAP Business ByDesign | SAP Help Portal

You can download the bot from the link: BOT Store

Below is an overview of the available bots and the benefits they offer.

1. Business Document Extraction from E-Mail

This bot is designed to automate the extraction of business documents from emails. It scans the relevant emails, downloads attachments, and categorizes them based on the document type and company code. Documents such as invoices are downloaded and stored in the user’s local or shared folders, based on predefined configuration parameters.

Business Benefits:

  • Reduces user effort by automating repetitive tasks.
  • Enhances efficiency by streamlining document handling processes.

2. Proof of Delivery Note Upload in Outbound Delivery and Invoice

This bot automates the upload of Proof of Delivery (POD) documents by extracting the delivery note number and attaching the documents to the corresponding outbound deliveries and customer invoices. By leveraging the SAP AI Business Services' Document Information Extraction, the bot ensures that each document is accurately matched and uploaded to the correct transaction.

Business Benefits:

  • Minimizes manual effort through automation.
  • Improves accuracy and reduces the risk of errors.
  • Enhances operational efficiency.

3. Scheduled Report Refresh

This bot automates the refreshing of SAP Business ByDesign Excel reports, ensuring that users have access to the latest data without manual intervention.

Business Benefits:

  • Reduces manual workload by automating report refreshes.
  • Improves productivity by ensuring timely access to updated data.

4. Sales Order Creation from Local Purchase Order

This bot automates the creation of sales orders from local purchase orders received from suppliers. It is capable of handling multiple purchase orders and company codes simultaneously. When used in conjunction with the Business Document Extraction bot, it provides seamless end-to-end automation, from document extraction to sales order creation.

Business Benefits:

  • Reduces manual workload by automating sales order creation.
  • Increases accuracy by minimizing the risk of manual input errors.
  • Improves operational efficiency across multiple company codes and documents.

5. Automated Purchase Order Upload

This bot automates the creation of purchase orders in SAP Business ByDesign using a predefined Excel template. It can work alongside the Business Document Extraction bot, automating the process from email receipt to purchase order creation, thus allowing for centralized bot management.

Business Benefits:

  • Reduces user effort through automated purchase order creation.
  • Enhances accuracy by minimizing manual data entry.
  • Improves efficiency through streamlined and automated processes.

6. Automated Stock Transfer Order (STO) Upload

This bot automates the creation of stock transfer orders from Excel files, facilitating the handling of large volumes of data with minimal manual intervention. It can operate as an independent bot, ensuring smooth stock transfers across the organization.

Business Benefits:

  • Reduces manual effort by automating large-scale data processing.
  • Improves efficiency and accuracy by automating stock transfer orders.

7. Automation of Opening and Closing Periods (2F2)

This bot automates the process of opening and closing financial periods across various company codes and sets of books. With simple configuration, it allows users to save time and avoid potential errors in repetitive financial tasks.

Business Benefits:

  • Increases accuracy by automating financial period management.
  • Saves time through automation, allowing users to focus on higher-value tasks.

8. Automated Supplier Invoice Upload (1R1)

This bot streamlines the upload of supplier invoices across multiple company codes. Utilizing the SAP Business ByDesign OData service, it ensures that scanned invoices are correctly uploaded and made visible in the Supplier Invoicing work center.

Business Benefits:

  • Reduces manual effort through automated invoice uploads.
  • Enhances accuracy by utilizing advanced scanning and OData services.
  • Improves operational efficiency by automating supplier invoice management.

Additional Automation Bots – Premium Solutions

1. Automated GR/IR Clearing

This bot automates the GR/IR clearing process by comparing goods or service receipts with their associated invoices. It clears the relevant accounts and handles any price, exchange rate, or payment discrepancies, ensuring accurate financial reporting.

Business Benefits:

  • Saves time by automating routine GR/IR clearing tasks.
  • Improves accuracy in financial reconciliation and reporting.

2. Automated WIP Clearing

This bot automates work-in-process (WIP) clearing runs, ensuring timely and accurate clearing of WIP accounts for completed production lots. It integrates with SAP Business ByDesign to manage production costs and financial postings more effectively.

Business Benefits:

  • Ensures reliable WIP clearing by automating the process multiple times daily.
  • Improves the accuracy of production and financial operations.

The introduction of these intelligent automation bots for SAP Business ByDesign offers substantial benefits, helping businesses reduce manual workloads, improve accuracy, and enhance overall efficiency. By automating repetitive and error-prone tasks, organizations can optimize their processes, allowing teams to focus on more strategic, value-adding activities, thus driving productivity and growth.

Useful KBAs:

3131444 - Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ) on Intelligent Robotic Process Automation (iRPA) for SAP Business ByDesign 

3302409 - Frequently Asked Question: iRPA Bot "Automated supplier invoice upload (1R1)"

3210095 - Frequently Asked Question: iRPA Bot "Business Document Extraction From Email"  

3200793 - Execution of BOTs Using APIs

entry.title: Learning and Enablement in SAP Projects
entry.content:
As businesses continue to navigate the complexities of digital transformation, effective project management and streamlined processes have become crucial. SAP Activate provides a comprehensive methodology to support this journey, and within this framework, the SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer stands out as an invaluable tool.

The SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer provides a variety of resources, such as accelerators, templates, and guides, that are relevant to each phase of an SAP project. These resources are designed to provide teams with knowledge and tools they need to execute their projects effectively, from planning to deployment and beyond. Specific content focusing on learning and enablement activities within SAP implementation projects is available in the SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer.

Some of the key learning and enablement deliverables include:

  • Enablement Strategy: Focusing on understanding the current learning and enablement capabilities (such as: is there an existing training and enablement team, tools and processes) to support a successful rollout of new learning and enablement plans. Defining enablement activities that are needed to support the project.
  • Learning Needs Analysis for Project Team: Identifying the project team’s learning needs and proposing learning paths based on their roles and the project’s objectives, evaluating knowledge gaps and recommending future learning paths.
  • Learning Needs Analysis for Users: Identifying learning and enablement requirements, analyzing digital learning opportunities, and thoroughly evaluating formal and informal ‘Push and Pull’ learning methodologies ensure that the right learning is available when needed.
  • Enablement Content Development: Creating readily accessible, easily digestible, and end-user specific enablement content ensures the effective adoption of solution knowledge and skills needed by the organization’s end users, contributing to higher system adoption and productivity.
  • Enablement Delivery: Covering the various methods of enablement delivery available to an organization, including onsite and virtual classroom learning, e-learning self-enablement, webinars, social collaboration, blogs, and on-the-job coaching.

This is a game-changer for project teams navigating the complexities of learning and enablement within SAP implementations. By providing easy access to curated, customizable resources, it empowers teams to work more efficiently, collaborate effectively, and reduce the risks associated with complex projects. Whether you are starting your SAP journey, or are deep into the deployment phase, the Roadmap Viewer’s learning and enablement content can help you achieve your enablement project goals with confidence.

entry.title: SAP ByD Error: System automatically links wrong purchase order ID to employee's time recording
entry.content:
Hi all,

we experience an error/ erroneous system behaviour that occurs with intercompany time recordings.
Before opening a ticket for the issue I thought maybe somebody here experienced the same and knows a solution.

Scenario:
We have an intercompany project between company A and company B (customer, sales order and project belong to company A). There is only one sales order position and one project task. From the project task we created a purchase order to allow colleagues from company B to support the project and record their time. For a while everything went well and we did not experience any problems.

Now the following error/ erroneous system behaviour occurs:
An employee from company A tries to record his time and recieves an error message stating that he is not part of the supplier company (company B) from the purchase order (original message in german: Lieferant DE01 (company B) von Bestellung 15730-1 stimmt nicht mit Unternehmen DE07 (company A) überein).
The error message is obviously true but I don't understand why the system tries to insert a purchase order ID in the first place since the employee belongs to company A.

What we already tried to solve the problem:
Since the system seems to be confused about the employees from different companies on the project task I opened another project task and only assigned one work package for the employee from company A. So all employees from company B are assigned to work packages below task 1 and only the employee from company A is assigned to a work package below task 2.
Unfortunately this only lead the system to throw another error message in addition, stating that the purchase order is linked to the other project task (original message in german: Bestellposition 15730-1 ist der anderen Projektaufgabe CPSO923-1 (Projektname) zugeordnet.)
Even though everything worked fine in the beginning I also re-checked the project, purchase order, sales order, and so on but could not find a solution/ a setting causing the problem.

Did anybody else experience this problem and found a solution already?
Any thoughts/ help would be highly appreciated!

entry.title: Re: Which SAP CAL appliance is best suited for installing...
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

We recommend you to try https://cal.sap.com/catalog#/applianceTemplates/80bb3343-9f04-4402-a339-76413795e182 (SAP ERP 6.0 EhP6 for Data Migration to SAP S/4HANA) Appliance Template. For more information, see the Appliance Template Getting Started Guide .

The flowing pages could be also helpful for you: https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/cloud-appliance-library/appliance-templates-faq/faq-licenses and https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-keep-an-expiring-trial-appliance-in-sap-cloud-appliance-library/ba-p/13327115 .

Best regards,

Tsvetinka

entry.title: How to influence pptx filename on SEN playback?
entry.content:
Hello,


Occasionally in SEN we include a pptx as a file.
We link to it from a book page.
When the pptx opens in playback it shows the generated filename in SEN rather than the actual filename that was uploaded.

I know we can upload pptxes into bookpages; I have encouraged the authors to do so but some insist on this approach to content production.

Grateful for any thoughts/suggestions.
Wallace

SAP Enable Now

entry.title: Re: S/4 HANA Product compliance - Safety Data Sheet creat...
entry.content:
Hello Shashi,

thanks for your interest in the new SAP S/4HANA for product compliance solution. 

You are right, the functionality to generate safety data sheets (SDS) is not available with the new product compliance solution in on-premise 2023 (OP2023). You can use the classic Report Management in the Product Safety module as alternative.

With CE2408 and OP2025 the basic functionality to generate safety data sheets (SDS) including a starter template will be available in the new solution. We plan to work on the data missing as input for the safety data sheet step by step, as well as on other important SDS topics like multi component products or branded products (see roadmap explorer).

For the SDS shipment you have two alternatives with OP2023. You can use the classic Report Shipping in Product Safety which is integrated in the classic logistics transactions. This is a valid option if you do not plan to use the new Fiori apps for logistics (mandatory for integration of the new dangerous goods solution into logistics).
As second option our solution supports a hybrid approach with the new product compliance solution. The shipping is then integrated in the new Fiori app for the goods issue. For the hybrid approach you will need to implement the synchronization of base safety data sheets via a Public API and implement a BAdI to generate the final SDS (including product and company specific data) with the classic generation approach via WWI. As a benefit of this approach you can receive and process requests from logistics, view information about the availability of safety data sheets for a single product in a world map, analyze the SDS availability, and monitor shipments.

I hope this helps.

Best regards,
Saskia Kalmbach (SAP)
Product Manager

entry.title: Re: Error when trying to create a scheduled publication in SAC
entry.content: for the stories that are affected - are you able to open the stories and manually refresh them?

entry.title: Wrapping / Truncate via Annotation?
entry.content:
We are currently CDS Annotations. However I was not able to find an annotation that allows me to set the wrapping / truncation parameters according to the Guidelines (https://experience.sap.com/fiori-design-web/wrapping-and-truncating-text/)

Is this possible without creating custom columns?

 

    @ui: {
        lineItem:[{ position: 20, importance: #HIGH, label: 'Inventurbezeichnung', cssDefault.width: '20rem'}],
        selectionField: [{ position: 20 }]
    }
    @ui.fieldGroup: [{label: 'Inventurbezeichnung'}]    
    @EndUserText.label: 'Inventurbezeichnung'
    Description;


Any input is much appreciated!

entry.title: Live Process Import Error
entry.content:
Importing a Live Process Project results in an error without any further information other than asking to raise an incident which I anyway plan to do, checking if anyone else has seen this error and suggest what possibly is going wrong or missing. 

Error: [customui] [ERROR] Deploying in org "dcd43088trial_sk-btp-kpqxpqu9" and space "SPACE"

Live Process Project being added: Project Approval and Release

Once added I am expecting to see this under Lobby but there is nothing visible, also what I have noticed is some service instances getting created under BTP related to this Process.

Thanks

Sreenivas

entry.title: App F3115: Prevent deletion of family members (HCM for S/4HANA)
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I have reviewed the app extensibility documentation but it does not seem to include a solution.

We are testing app F3115 "My Family Members (Fiori 2.0)" in an HCM for S/4HANA environment.

We want to prevent the ESS user from creating and deleting family members. Is there a simple way to do this?

We are already using SM31 view V_T588MUIFPROPC to set certain fields to read-only, but that's pretty pointless as long as the user can just delete the entire record. I could also not find any enhancement options in the "Adapt UI" functionality of the FLP.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud Configuration to Import Data from...
entry.content:
"Authentication Failed: PKIX path building failed":
This points to an issue with SSL/TLS certificate validation.
The error means that SAC cannot verify the certificate provided by the server (API) because the required intermediate or root certificate is missing from the trust store.

If the certificate chain is missing a trusted root or intermediate certificate, you’ll need to import these certificates into the SAC trust store.

Obtain the necessary certificates (usually available from the API provider or extracted via a tool like OpenSSL)

If you're a SAC administrator - import the missing certificates into SAC's trust store.

entry.title: Re: Adding new tab @ Delivery Header for Tcode VL02N (/ VL01 / VL03)
entry.content:
Hi,

how tho change SCREEN attributes when clicking on the "Display <-> Change" button? I can set the INPUT attribute to either 0 or 1 based on the T-CODE, but the T-CODE does not change once you're inside even if you switch from Display to Change or viceversa.

entry.title: Re: sap apo snp: Version A of DataStore object 9ASNPBAS does not exist
entry.content: Got any response for the above?

entry.title: Re: Time Sheet: Approved time sheet should not be changed by emps (Only Admin/Manager should)
entry.content:
Hi Volker,

If within 'recording admissibility' (not outside) period, any weekly timesheet is approved and before payroll run, business don't want emp to change further without admin/manager intervention, we need to put this validation check.

Hope, I am clear to you now. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Technical details - HANA licensing
entry.content:
Hi Jens,

We want to check SOD conflicts using web application, we export the users and roles data using ABAP program from S4 system. Those files being read by third party web application to check the SOD conflicts. 

We don't update back anything to SAP system . 

Does this require additional license or indirect license?

Also , if we create users in SAP system by reading the csv file require any license?

Thanks

Srikanth

entry.title: Re: Create task in My Timesheet for EPPM Projects for uns...
entry.content: Work Packages only refer to Customer Projects.  Use the Other Accounting Object option and select an Enterprise project WBS.

entry.title: Re: SAC - Calculated Measure
entry.content:
Does this work?

IF([d/Cost_Unit].[p/Fixed_Variable] = "Var",
[Value] * LOOKUP([Avg_Participants], [Course Dimension]),
[Value])

entry.title: Re: Only able to successfully call api '/raylight/v1/abou...
entry.content: Yes, after you have logged in and received the LogonToken (response from the logon call)), you should include the token in the “X-SAP-LogonToken” header on every RESTful call.

entry.title: Re: Link Sales Order & Purchase Order in SAP Standard Rep...
entry.content: You can check in me2k with the related Account Assignment Category used in PO.

entry.title: Re: Task 3 - Enhancing CDS View Entities with Annotations
entry.content: Thanks!

entry.title: Re: How do I pass the raw data as content for Attachment...
entry.content: Hi Gregorw, Thanks for your reply. I am using Neptune DXP open edition tool for developing the UI. I created server script with the below logic and it worked const { image, slug, contentType, BusinessObjectTypeName, LinkedSAPObjectKey } = req.body; log.info(req.body); // Create a Buffer from a Base64 encoded image string. // The `Buffer.from()` method converts the Base64 string into binary data that can be used as the body of a request. var buffer = Buffer.from(image, "base64"); const opts = { headers: { Slug: "script6_file.jpeg", "Content-Type": "image/jpeg", BusinessObjectTypeName: BusinessObjectTypeName, LinkedSAPObjectKey: LinkedSAPObjectKey, }, body: buffer, }; console.log(opts); //complete(); try { // Send api request. const response = await apis.AttachmentContentSet(opts);

entry.title: Re: Adobe LiveCycle Designer : «there is insufficient space for the inserted column».
entry.content:
Very helpful and detailed description. Thank you.
But I have the problem that the funcition "Convert to Subform" is greyed out.
My ADLC version is 11.0

Any Idea why I cannot use this function?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: How to calculate the time difference between the same...
entry.content:
The example you gave has no repetitions with the same actual procedure within 48 hours, so I added some cases that meet the condition, see my example.

The solution I proposed assumed that the patient ID is identical to the case number and the repetitions are the "actual procedure"

entry.title: Re: Please correct the quantity: Quantity on asset 1000 90000248-0, ledger 0L is only 0
entry.content: Hi Mofty, I saw you created an incident. The scenario is complex since you have more than on receiver asset with the quantity to be divided, develop will work on a solution.

entry.title: Re: GST Margin scheme on second hand material purchases
entry.content: There should be some logic to arrive this margin scheme.  So have another condition type to capture the margin scheme value before tax condition type.  Depending upon the logic, maintain the condition record.

entry.title: Re: Interact programmatically with SAP CAP framework's No...
entry.content:
The cds bind command was the missing piece! Thanks Gregor!

Using it, profile selection and binding resolution don't have to be part of my script logic anymore. Only had to change how I call my script, not from within my subfolder anymore, but from my CAP modules' root folder.

# Execute in CAP module root (where your package.json is)
cds bind --profile hybrid-dev --exec -- node migration/cap-startup-test2.js
//file: migration/cap-startup-test2.js
const cds = require('@sap/cds')

async function capStartup() {
const app = cds.app
cds.emit('bootstrap', app)

// load model from all sources
const csn = await cds.load('*').then(cds.minify)
cds.model = cds.compile.for.nodejs(csn)
cds.emit('loaded', cds.model)

// connect to prominent required services
if (cds.requires.db) cds.db = await cds.connect.to('db')
if (cds.requires.messaging) await cds.connect.to('messaging')

// serve own services as declared in model
cds.serve('all').from(csn).in(app)
await cds.emit('served', cds.services)
}

async function main() {
// Startup CAP Framework
await capStartup()

// Do your thing
const { MyEntity } = cds.db.entities('my.namespace')
console.log(await SELECT.from(MyEntity).columns('count(ID) as cnt'))

// Shutdown CAP Framework
cds.exit()
}

main()

entry.title: Re: Duplicate Inspection lot creation on Sales Return Ins...
entry.content:
Next check: did somebody move the 08 inspection type to another origin? SPRO>Quality Management>Quality inspection>Maintain Inspection Lot Origins and Assign Inspection Types

Origin 05 standard looks like this:

entry.title: Re: How do I trigger a User Defined Object in PostTransactionNotice
entry.content:
Hi,

I'd like to share an example of a query for postransactionnotice I have ever made on April 18, 2012 ago.

/* customized date setting in UDF day name, dd-mmm-yyyy */
-- result example : Wednesday, 18-May-2012
If @object_type = '67' and @transaction_type in ('A','U')

Begin

declare @day as varchar(30)
declare @date1 as varchar(30)
declare @Hari as nvarchar(30)
set @day = (SELECT distinct
case
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Monday' then cast('Monday' as nvarchar(20))
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Tuesday' then cast('Tuesday' as nvarchar(20))
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Wednesday' then cast('Wednesday' as nvarchar(20))
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Thursday' then cast('Thursday' as nvarchar(20))
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Friday' then cast('Friday' as nvarchar(20))
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Saturday' then cast('Saturday' as nvarchar(20))
when DATENAME(dw, (T0.[DocDate])) = 'Sunday' then cast('Sunday' as nvarchar(20))
end from OWTR t0 where t0.Docentry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del)
set @date1 = (select distinct
cast(day(a.[DocDate]) as nvarchar(20))+'-'+case
when month(a.docdate) = '1' then cast('Jan' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '2' then cast('Feb' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '3' then cast('Mar' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '4' then cast('Apr' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '5' then cast('May' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '6' then cast('Jun' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '7' then cast('Jul' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '8' then cast('Aug' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '9' then cast('Sep' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '10' then cast('Oct' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '11' then cast('Nov' as nvarchar(20))
when month(a.docdate) = '12' then cast('Dec' as nvarchar(20))
end+'-'+ cast(year(a.[DocDate]) as nvarchar(20))
FROM Owtr a WHERE a.docentry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del)

update OWTR
set U_SI_HARI_TGL_KIRIM = (@day+','+' '+@date1)
where @object_type = '67'
and DocEntry = @List_of_cols_val_tab_del

end

 

so, the postransactionnotice works when I add or update the transfer document. So, you must make sure that you use a valid document to update the custom tables.

if not, it won't works.

 

Rgds,

Jimmy

entry.title: Re: How do I pass the raw data as content for Attachment...
entry.content: It's too bad that comments do not allow nice formatting. I would suggest you ask in the Neptune Forums instead.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere - public vs private tenants
entry.content: Thanks for the reply. Indeed our SAP CSM also confirmed that there is no private (dedicated) option for SAP Datasphere.

entry.title: Re: Decrypting SAP IBP Optimiser Logs
entry.content:
You can try the below

- Make sure that the Input/Output Files are TEXT files, alternatively, you can directly update the path in cells 13,8 and 19,8

Regards

Ajay

entry.title: Re: Interact programmatically with SAP CAP framework's No...
entry.content: Great that it helped. Please vote for the influencing request I've created for this issue: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/330208
